calendar.matters: STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Continued To May-06-27 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The Status Conference re: Final Accounting is CONTINUED TO MAY 6, 2027, AT 9:00 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT.

Plaintiff's counsel must file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing, with appropriate evidentiary support, no later than ten court days before the continued hearing, with courtesy copy provided to this department within 24 hours of filing. If all funds have not been accounted for and distributed by the date of the status report, all counsel shall appear in person at the May 6, 2027 hearing.

It appears Plaintiff is in violation of a court order. In connection with the February 10, 2026 hearing, the court ordered the following: "Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing." Further, a similar directive is set out in Local Rule 2.7. Three months later, the court is without the proposed order. Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order quoting verbatim the tentative ruling for the 2/10/2026 hearing; the proposed order must be emailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 5/11/2026.

Additionally, it appears-as was so in connection with the 2/10/2026 hearing-Plaintiff violated the court's order directing him to promptly deliver to the department a courtesy copy of the report filed in connection with the hearing. Plaintiff must comply and confirm receipt.

Subsequent violations of written orders will result in an OSC and an order requiring counsel to appear in person to explain their failure to comply.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. TO BE CLEAR, PLAINTIFF IS TO SUBMIT PROMPTLY TWO PROPOSED ORDERS.

The Court signs two orders. (see scanned orders for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Jun-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Continued To May-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The Status Conference re: Final Accounting is CONTINUED TO MAY 12, 2026, AT 9:00 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT.

Plaintiffs' counsel must file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing, with appropriate evidentiary support, no later than ten court days before the continued hearing, with courtesy copy provided to this department within 24 hours of filing. The court notes that it ordered Plaintiff to promptly deliver to the department a courtesy copy of the report filed in connection with this hearing and the court never received it. Violations of court orders are not acceptable. It is up to Plaintiff to comply and confirm receipt.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2026 Continued To Mar-25-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Christopher Garcia, Esq. 213-232-3128 Moon Law Group for Plaintiff.
Henry Platt. Esq. 202-342-3447 Saul Ewing LLP 202-342-3447 for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

Hearing is held. The Court comments - casual view of implementation of settlement created complications which court will not resolve. Mr. Garcia responds and presents timeline to the court. Mr. Platt presents arguments; took a year to do this, seek modification of agreement, no one prejudiced.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING.

Continued to 2/10/26 at 9am in Dept. 302. Plaintiff to file status report no later than 1/27/26 with hard Courtesy copies to the department within 24 hours of lodging for filing of documents; at close of business on 1/28/26. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Continued To Dec-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FINAL ACCOUNTING.

Continued to December 17, 2025. Plaintiff was ordered to submit a final report including the actual amounts paid to the PAGA Settlement Group Members, the other amounts distributed under the Settlement Agreement, and the amounts tendered to the State of California, Unclaimed Property division at least nine (9) court days prior to the hearing. Plaintiff failed to submit a report and is ordered to submit a final report at least nine days prior to December 17, 2025. Plaintiff is required to file supply courtesy copies per SF Local Rule 2.7B.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Christopher Garcia, Esq. (213) 232-3128 MOON LAW GROUP, PC for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is not contested. Mr. Garcia clarifies and requests a compliance date/status conference re: final accounting. The Court sets 10/21/25 at 9am in Dept. 302. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF SHAY-RYAN JONES' Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement Agreement.

Plaintiff Shay-Ryan Jones' motion for approval of PAGA settlement is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The court will sign the proposed order previously submitted by plaintiff.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Nov-14-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS RHMT, LLC and STAT TEAM, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendants' (RHMT, LLC and STAT Team, Inc.) motion to compel arbitration of Plaintiff's individual Private Attorney General Act (PAGA) claim is granted. Defendants' motion to dismiss non-individual PAGA claims is denied. The non-individual PAGA claims are stayed pending the California Supreme Court's decision in Adolph v. Uber Technologies, Inc.

[see word minutes for details.]

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
